title: Issue No. 5
preview: Today: Glossier's quiet rebrand is louder than they think, the biotech ingredient turning up in every "clean" moisturizer this spring, a runway lip from Marni SS26 that made me gasp audibly, and why Dress for Success deserves your attention this International Women's Week.

title: Issue No. 4
preview: Today: Glossier's quiet war with its own subreddit, the polyglutamic acid moment nobody asked for but everybody needs, Dries Van Noten's final bow at AW26, and a charity putting lipstick in the hands of women rebuilding after incarceration. Happy International Women's Day — let's get into it.

title: Issue No. 3
preview: Today: Drunk Elephant's messy reformulation drama, the biotech peptide that's quietly replacing retinol in clinical trials, Dries Van Noten's final collection moment that made three editors cry, and why Look Good Feel Better deserves your attention this weekend.

title: Issue No. 2
preview: Mascara that doesn't flake is apparently still a pipe dream — but one lab in Seoul thinks they've cracked it. Also: Charlotte Tilbury's latest acquisition is either brilliant or very, very worrying. We've got the scent ingredient your nervous system actually likes, Valentino's lip moment from Milan, and a woman who makes wigs for cancer patients out of hair she collects from salon floors.
